def: s.m. A log tied to the neck of a vicious ox ( = ḍholā).
v.t. (Braj) = denā.
s.m. See s.v. den.
s.m. (dialec.) = ḍāṅṭh, ḍanṭhā, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. deva or dev.
s.m. Mound; high ground.
s.m. = dev-nal, q.v.s.v. deva or dev.

ety: fr. दिवड्ढओ = Prk. अड्ढदुइअओ = S. अर्ध+द्वितीय+कः
def: s.m. Reckoning by ḍeṛh; half as much again; interest in kind at fifty per cent. (esp. on grain, a maund and a half of seed being repaid for a maund taken)
subs: v.t. To make one and a half times as much or as great; — to balance account books, &c., close an account
s.m. An old system of account during native rule, under which interest was not decreed when it had exceeded fifty per cent. of the principal.

ety: ḍeʼoṛhā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To take once and a half as much; to receive back the sum lent with fifty per cent. interest.
s.f. Threshold, porch, ante-chamber, entrance, door
s.f. = ḍeʼoṛhī, q.v.
s.f. A tooth, &c. ( = ḍāṛh, q.v.)
subs: ḍeṛ mārnā or deṛeṅ mārnā = ḍāṛh mārnā

def: adj. = next, q.v.

ety: old P. drañga; Pehl. darg, der; Zend daregha, fr. darez = S. दीर्घ, rt. दर्ह् (दृह्)
def: adj. & adv. Long, of long standing or time, old, antique; — late, long since, a long while, a while since; tardily, slowly; — s.f. A long time, a period of time, an interval; lateness, delay, tardiness, slowness
subs: prov. What is long in coming, or happening, or doing, will be well come, or well done
adj. Durable, lasting, firm, constant; — slow-paced
or talak, adv. For a long time, till late
v.n. To delay, to be a long time, be behind time
adv. Always; der lagānā, v.n. To delay, loiter, tarry, be tardy
adv. Late, after a long time or interval.

ety: P. ديره dera; prob. = dehrā, q.v.
def: s.m. House, habitation, dwelling, tent ( = ḍerā); an idol-temple ( = dehrā).
s.m. Tent, marquee, pavilion, temporary dwelling; abiding place, dwelling, house
subs: adj. Having a fixed abode, well-to-do (as a courtezan, &c.)
To pitch a tent; to encamp; to take up (one's) abode; to tarry, linger, loiter, delay
To erect or pitch a tent; to encamp
v.n. Tent to be pitched; — to be encamped

def: s.m. Tent, marquee, pavilion, temporary dwelling; abiding place, dwelling, house
subs: adj. Having a fixed abode, well-to-do (as a courtezan, &c.)
To pitch a tent; to encamp; to take up (one's) abode; to tarry, linger, loiter, delay
To erect or pitch a tent; to encamp
v.n. Tent to be pitched; — to be encamped

def: adj. Squinting, squint-eyed (cf. ḍherā).

ety: for दिवड्ढो, fr. Prk. अड्ढदुइओ = S. अर्ध+द्वितीयः
def: adj. One and a half
subs: 'The gentleman is in his garden, which consists of but a tree and a half of the bakāyan kind'; the saying is applied to a person who assumes consequence without reason
s.m. A weight equal to three-eighths of a ser
s.m. A kind of embroidered stuff worn by women (the embroidery being on the front, and a little on the sides)
s.m. A ser and a half
s.m. Half a bow-tie; a slip-knot; a knot that is easily opened
apnī ḍeṛh īṅṭ-kī masjid banānā or ćunnā
'To make a mosque for oneself of a brick and a half'; to withdraw, through pride, from the society of others; to have ideas or opinions of one's own.

def: s.f. Lateness, delay, &c. ( = der, q.v.)
subs: v.n. To delay, to dally; to be a long while
(-ko), To be late; to be long; to be slow, &c.

r: derīn, dīrīn, derīna, dīrīna, der, īn, īna, aêna
ety: der, q.v.+īn or īna = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Old, ancient; experienced, wise; shrewd, cunning; — derīna, s.m. An old or experienced person.

r: derīna, dīrīna, derīn, dīrīn, der, īn, īna, aêna
ety: der, q.v.+īn or īna = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Old, ancient; experienced, wise; shrewd, cunning; — derīna, s.m. An old or experienced person.
s.m. Place, spot, country, &c. ( = deś, q.v.); — 2˚ A kind of rāg sung at midnight (cf. deśākh and deśkār).
s.m. An annual offering for the dead (among Mohammadans).
s.m. Place, spot, region, country, territory, province, dominion; part, side, direction
subs: (˚śa+aṭ˚), s.m. The wandering through a country or countries, peregrination
(˚śa+āć˚), s.m. Custom or usage of a country, local usage or custom
(˚śa+adh˚), s.m. (f. -patnī); and deśādhikarī (˚śa+adh˚), s.m. (f. -inī), The ruler of a country, governor of a province, ruler, governor, monarch
(˚śa+an˚), s.m. The end, limit, or frontier of a country
(˚śa+an˚), s.m. Another country, foreign country, abroad
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Of or belonging to another or foreign country; — a foreigner
To go from country to country, to travel about
vulg. des-bhākhā, s.f. Vernacular language, local dialect
s.m. The abandoning one's country, voluntary exile, emigration
s.m. One who abandons his country, an emigrant, exile
vulg. des-dharam, s.m.The law or usage of a country, local law, local rights or customs
s.m. Many countries, different or various countries; — adv. From country to country; through various lands
v.t. To banish from a country
vulg. des-wāl, s.m. (f. -ī), A native of a country
(˚śa+up˚), s.m. National misfortune or calamity, national distress
s.f. The laws of a country
vulg. des-vyahār, and des-beʼohār, s.m. Custom or practice of a country or province, local usage
(˚śa+aiś˚), s.m. The greatness or magnificence of a country.

def: s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode sung at noon in the spring.
s.f. corr. of daśārṇā, q.v.
s.m.: For words beginning with des˚ which are not given here, see under the form deś.
s.m. = H ديساوري देसावरी desāwarī, adj. & s.f. See disāwar; and disāwarī.
= H ديشاوري देशावरी deśāvarī, see disāwar; and disāvarī.
adj. & s.f. = H ديساور देसावर desāwar, s.m. See disāwar; and disāwarī.
= H ديشاور देशावर deśāvar, see disāwar; and disāvarī.
s.f. Appearance.
adj. Of or belonging to a country, native, indigenous; home-made; local; provincial; — s.m. Native of a country.
adj. Of or belonging to a country, native, indigenous, local, provincial; — deśī, s.m. A native of a country (see desī).
s.f. The index or fore-finger.
adj. Of or belonging to a country, native, indigenous, local, provincial; — deśī, s.m. A native of a country (see desī).

r: deś-kār, deskār, deś-kārī, deskārī, rāg, rāginī
def: s.m. = S ديشکاري देशकारी deś-kārī, vulg.deskārī, s.f. Name of a musical mode (rāg, and rāginī) sung in the morning.

r: deś-kārī, deskārī, deś-kār, deskār, rāg, rāginī
def: s.f. = S ديشکار देशकार deś-kār, vulg. deskār,s.m. Name of a musical mode (rāg, and rāginī) sung in the morning.
s.m. (Braj) = deś, q.v.
s.m. = deś-wālā, q.v.s.v. deś.
adj. = deśīya, q.v.; — s.m. An eye-witness (of anything); — an inhabitant or native of a country.

ety: Pehl. dev; Zend daêva, fr. dīv; S. देव
def: s.m. An evil spirit, devil, demon, an evil jinn, a ghost, hobgoblin; a giant, a monster, a huge fellow or thing
subs: vulg. deʼo-bād, s.f. lit. 'A devil's wind,' a whirlwind, 'a devil'
vulg. deʼo-stān, s.m. The habitation of demons
s.m. A perfect giant, a veritable monster.

def: s.m. A deity, a god ( = dev).
s.f. The plant Hibiscus mutabilis; and Marcilea quadrifolia.
s.m. Giving, paying, &c.; — a giver (e.g. pānī-dewā)
subs: s.m., or dewā-leʼī, s.f. Barter, traffic, &c. ( = den-len, q.v.s.v. den).

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A deity, god; — an evil spirit, a demon (in this sence, com. deʼo cf. P. dev) — a divine man, a Brāhman, a priest (applied as a surname to Brahmans and to Kāyaths); a god among men, a king, prince; — a husband's brother (cf. devar)
subs: (˚va+ālaya), s.m. 'Residence of the gods,' a division of heaven; — an idol temple, a pagoda
(˚va+āh˚), s.f. An oblation to the gods
s.m. The tree Rottleria tinctoria (used in dyeing)
s.m. 'Residence of the gods,' heaven, paradise; a temple; the holy fig-tree
s.m. 'Lord of the gods,' an epithet of Indra
s.m. The way of the gods, the celestial path or way, the firmament, heaven
s.f. Worship of the gods, worship of idols, idolatry, paganism
s.m. (f. -ikā), A worshipper of the gods or of idols, an idolater, pagan
(S. deva-tāḍa), s.m. The plant Luffa fætida; the grass Andropogon serratus

s.m. 'Tree of the gods,' the holy fig-tree; the tree of plenty (one of the trees of Svarg or paradise); the old tree of a village (held sacred by the villagers), the tree under which (or the place in which) villagers assemble
(S. deva-sthāna), s.f.m.(?) A place of idols, an idol temple; — (met.) the rain
s.m. The part of the hands sacred to the gods, i.e. the tips of the fingers
vulg. deʼo-dār, s.m. A species of pine, the cedar, Pinus deodora or deodār; — (in Bengal) the tree Uvaria longifolia ( = debdār); — (in the peninsula) the tree Erythrosylon sideroxyloides
s.f. A species of creeper (com. called hatī-ghosh)
(f. -ā), vulg. deʼo-datt, adj. Given by the gods, god-given; given to the gods; — s.m. (f. -ā), Gift of God; a proper name ( = ḵẖudā-dād)
dev-dūtak, s.m. Messenger of the gods, divine envoy, angel
s.m. 'Grain of the gods,' a kind of grain cultivated in many parts of Hindūstān, Andropogon saccharatus, A. or Holcus sorghum ( = joʼār)
s.f. A name of the Ganges
s.m. A fragrant resin or bdellium used for incense

s.m. lit. 'God-given'; the name given to Śuna-śeph after being received into the family of Viśvā-mitra; — name of a king who was the son of Su-ketu and descendant of Nimi; — name of a king who was the son of Karambhi; — an epithet of Rājā Parikshit; — a sort of crane
s.m. Divine ruler, king of the gods, an epithet of Indra
devarshi, s.m. A sage or saint of the class of demi-gods, a divine sage (as Nārad, Atri, Marīći, &c.)
(f. -ā), or dev-rūpī (f. -inī), adj. Of divine form, god-like
s.m. 'Property of the gods,' property applicable to religious purposes, religious endowments
s.m. A species of pine, Pinus Devadāru
s.m. 'Divine paste,' a fragrant paste of sandal, agallochum, camphor, and safflower
s.m. Religious act or rite, worship of the gods, oblation to the gods
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode
s.m. 'The flower of the gods'; cloves
s.f. A celestial or divine maiden, a nymph
s.f. One of the Rāginīs or female personifications of the modes of music
s.m. 'Songster of the gods,' a celestial chorister ( = gandharv)
s.m. 'House of the gods'; a celestial or planetary sphere; — a temple, oratory; palace of a king
or dev-girī, s.f. One of the Rāginīs or female personifications of the modes of music (cf. dev-kirī)

vulg. deʼo-lok, s.m. Heaven, paradise; the particular sphere or heaven of any divinity; any one of the seven superior worlds (from the earth to the highest or Satyalok, in opposition to those below the earth)
To go to paradise, to die
s.m. 'The divine month, or month of the gods'; the eighth month of pregnancy
s.m. The residence or dwelling of the gods
s.m. = deva-nāgarī, vulg. dev-nāgrī, s.f. lit. 'The divine city writing'; the character in which Sanskrit is usually written (also termed nāgarī)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), God-made, god-created, natural; — any created or natural object
s.m. A kind of reed, Arundo bengalensis (the stem of which is made into writing pens)
s.f. Reviling the gods, contemptuous speech in reference to the gods, blasphemy, infidelity, atheism, heresy
s.m. A reviler of the gods, atheist, unbeliever, heretic
vulg. dev-bānī, s.f. Divine voice, voice from heaven; language of the gods; the Sanskrit language
(˚va+ut˚), vulg. deʼo-uṭhān, deʼo-ṭhān, s.m. The awaking and rising of Vishnu on the eleventh of the bright half of Kārttik
(˚va+ud˚), s.m. 'Grove of the gods,' a sacred grove, a garden near a temple or consecrated to a deity
s.m. A divine boon or blessing
s.m. A vow or obligation to any deity

vulg. deʼo-br̤iksh, s.m. 'The divine tree'; name of the Mandāra and other fabulous trees of heaven or paradise; — the tree Echites (or Alstonia) scholaris; — a plant yielding a fragrant resin or bdellium
adj. Invoking the gods, — s.m. The left ear (which is turned northwards if the face is directed towards the east)
s.f. Invocation of a deity; name of a magical formula with which the gods are invoked; — name of a daughter of Manu Svayam-bhū and wife of Kardam
s.m. The car or vehicle of a god
s.f. Name of a daughter of Uśanas, wife of Yayāti and mother of Yadu and Turvasu
(˚va+ai˚), s.m. The dominion of the gods
(˚va+in˚), s.m. 'Chief of the gods,' an epithet of Indra; and of Śiva.

def: s.m. A giver, bestower ( = dene-wālā).
adj. (f. -ikā), God-like, divine, celestial; — s.m. Name of the inhabitants of one of the Varshas or divisions of the world.
s.f. Daughter of Devak, wife of Vasu-dev and mother of Krishna
subs: s.m. 'Devakī's joy,' or 'the son of Devakī,' an epithet of Krishna.

def: adj. Belonging or relating to a divinity; divine, godlike.
s.f. = duʼal, diwāl, or diwālī, q.v.
s.m. = dewal, and devālay, qq.v.

ety: dewā, q.v.+al = Prk. अडो = S. अ+लः
def: adj. & s.m. Giving, liberal, generous ( = dān-śīl); able to give or pay, solvent; — a giver, a liberal man, &c.
s.m. An idol temple, a small temple or pagoda.
s.m. An attendant upon an idol.
s.m. 1˚ = devālay; — 2˚ = diwālā, qq.v.
s.f. 1˚ = devālay, q.v ; — 2˚ = dewā-leʼī, q.v.s.v. dewā.
s.m. See s.v. deva, dev.
s.f. A small temple or building made for an idol only, a shrine, an altar.
s.m. = diwāliyā, q.v.
v.t. (prov.) = dilānā, q.v.
s.m. A husband's brother (esp. his younger brother).
contrac. of next, q.v. (and = ḍerh, q.v.).
s.m. A religious rite, offering oblations to the gods, worship of the gods, any observance due to a deity from a worshipper.

s.f. State or nature of a deity, divinity, divine character, divine dignity or power; — (in Hindī also) dew-tā or deʼotā (cf. daivat), s.m.f. A Hindū deity, a god or goddess, idol or image of a god; — an object of worship, or which is held sacred, a snake, &c.; — divine being, holy man, good man, gentleman; (in respectful address to a religious superior, &c.) 'your Holiness,' 'your Reverence,' 'your Lordship'
subs: (˚tā+an˚), s.m.f. Another god; — adv. In the midst of the gods.

def: s.f. A goddess.
s.f. A sort of gull, Larus ridibundus.
s.m. Divine character, divinity, &c. ( = devatā, q.v.).
s.m. See s.v. deva or dev.
s.f. = dev-girī, q.v.s.v. deva, dev.
s.m. An idol temple.
s.f. A female deity, a goddess; the wife of a deity, (especially) Durgā or Bhawānī (wife of Śiva); — a queen; the plant Trigonella corniculata (and several other plants)
subs: s.m. The husband of a goddess, (especially) an epithet of Śiva
s.m. The city of Vān, probably 'Davicotta,' on the Coromandel coast.

def: s.f. = dīwlī, q.v.
s.f. A fish-scale; scale, scab, scar, cicatrix.
s.m. = donā or daunā, q.v.
s.f. A female deʼo or demon.
s.m. = S ديووتهان देवोत्थान devotthān, s.m. See s.v. deva, dev, and cf. deʼo-ṭhān.
s.m. = ? ديووديان देवोद्यान devodyān, s.m. See s.v. deva, dev, and cf. deʼo-ṭhān.
adj. = debrā, q.v.
s.f. Husband's younger brother's wife.
s.f. The eleventh day of the light fortnight of the month Kārttik (Oct.-Nov.), on which the god Vishṇu awakes from his sleep of four months.

ety: dew ( = dīwā = S. दीप)+ṭī = aṭī = Prk. अडिआ = S. ट ( = र)+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.), A lamp; a torch.
adj. To be given or presented, to be granted or allowed; fit or proper to be given, due; proper for a gift.
s.m. The letter or sound dha.
s.m. = dhap, and dhab-dhab, qq.v.

ety: prob. S. धर्मः; cf. ḍaul, and ḍhāl
def: s.m. Shape, form, fashion; mode, manner, way, style; idiom; knack; habit; course, means; position; opportunity; manners, breeding, behaviour, conduct; know. ledge, savoir faire, skilful management, art, dexterity, address; — a small light hammer (syn. hathauṭī)
subs: (-ke), To be moulded or fashioned to (one's) will, to be gained or won by skilful management, to be brought under the influence or power (of), to be caught or taken by artful management, to fall into (one's) clutches
adj. Well-formed, well-shaped ( = ḍhabīlā), graceful; fine, showy
(-kā), To set the fashion (of), to make a habit (of), get into a manner or way; to accustom, train (to)
adj. (f. -ī), In proper form, suitable, appropriate; moderate; reasonable; skilful, dexterous
v.n. Some way or means to be found, a chance or opportunity to arise, to get an opportunity; to obtain an advantage (over), get the upper hand (of).

def: s.m. A flat roof of earth; a house so roofed.
s.m. A net; — the eaves of a house (extending three or four feet beyond the wall, so that people may sit under them); (local) a large kind of tile, ridge-tile.
adj. = dhabbal, q.v.
s.f. A big fat woman; — a very full skirt or petticoat.
adj. Slushy, muddy, watery, turbid, thick
subs: adj. Watery, slushy, &c.; sodden, insipid (as food).

def: (from ḍhānā), s.f.(?), Rubbish, filth, dirt.

ety: prob. = thappā; cf. dhap, dhappā; thāp, thappaṛ, and ṭāp
def: s.m. A coloured mark or patch, a stain, a blot; stigma, brand, slur

ety: prob. dhab or dhap, q.v.+Prk. आलो = S. आलः
def: adj. & s.m. Heavy, large, thick-set, stout, sturdy, fat, corpulent; — a thick-set or sturdy fellow, a fat man, &c.
s.m. = next, q.v.
adj. Dumpy, podgy, heavy, fat, corpulent; clumsy, awkward.
adj. (dialec.) = ḍhabar-ḍhabar, q.v.
s.m. Sound of footsteps; stamping, heavy tramp or tread; — adv. Falling with a noise or thud; stamp-stamp, tramp-tramp, thud thud.
s.m. Foster-brother.
adj. (f. -ī), Well-formed, well-shaped, well-made, graceful, elegant (syn. su-ḍaul; sajīlā).
s.f. The log connecting the rati and beam to which the yoke is attached in an oil-mill, &c.
s.m. A coarse and strong garment or cloth, a stout dhotī, any loose garment for covering the legs, a skirt, a petticoat.
adj. Slushy, muddy, watery, turbid, thick
subs: adj. Watery, slushy, &c.; sodden, insipid (as food).

ety: dhābā, q.v.+rī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A fowl-house; pigeon-house, dove-cot.

ety: S. स्तम्ब+कः (with w or u inserted)
def: s.m. lit. 'A small lump'; an unstamped lump of copper used as money, and equal in value to a paisā; (fig.) money, wealth (also written ḍhebuʼā).
s.m. corr. of dabūs, q.v.
v.n. To be jerked; to jolt, to shake; to give way, to sink (as a bog, slough, &c.).
adj. & s.m. Fragile, weak, infirm, rickety, loose; gimcrack; — frame, frame-work, plan, &c. ( = ḍhāṅćā, q.v.)
subs: ḍhaćar khaṛā karnā, To form a plan, to frame, plan; to make a show, keep up appearances
(-kā), To make preparations (for), make arrangements (for), provide necessary apparatus (for).

def: s.m. Jolt, jerk, shock; loss, damage ( = dhakkā, q.v.)
subs: dhaćkā lagnā, v.n. To experience a shock, suffer loss, or damage.

def: s.f. Plan, contrivance, device, artifice, trick.
s.m. (dialec.) = dhāṛā; dhārā; dhār, qq.v.
s.m. Low ground where water collects or lies ( = ḍabrā, ḍābar, q.v.).
s.f. = ḍhaḍḍā, q.v.
s.f. A species of babbler, Malacocercus malcolmi (a chattering gregarious bird, commonly seen in flocks of seven or more, and hence called 'the seven sisters'); — a decrepit old woman, a harridan.

s.m. lit. 'Burnt letters'; in versification, certain words or letters which are considered by Hindī poets to be unlucky (as ह, ग, न at the beginning of a verse; र, ज, स in the middle; and क, ट, ज्ञ at the end).

ety: dhadhak˚ = Prk. दद्धक्क(इ), or दद्धक्के(इ), fr. S. दग्ध (p.p. of rt. दह्)+कृ
def: v.n. To burn fiercely, to blaze, to flare.
adj. (f. -ī) Blazing, burning fiercely, flaring.
v.n. = dhadhaknā, q.v.

ety: prob. a redupl. form of ḍhūṅḍhnā
def: v.t. To search narrowly, to ransack, rummage; to seek; to investigate.

ety: Prk. दद्ध = S. दग्ध (p.p. of rt. दह्)+कारः
def: s.m. Igniting, setting fire to, burning.
s.f. Sound, noise, cry, &c. ( = dhāh, q.v.)
subs: s.f. Noise, clatter, report (of cannon), distant booming (of guns, &c.), sound of a drum
v.n. To sound, to make a noise or clatter, to resound, to boom; to jabber, prate; to wrangle, quarrel.

def: s.f. Sound, noise, cry, &c. ( = dhāh, q.v.)
subs: s.f. Noise, clatter, report (of cannon), distant booming (of guns, &c.), sound of a drum
v.n. To sound, to make a noise or clatter, to resound, to boom; to jabber, prate; to wrangle, quarrel.

ety: prob. fr. S. धा; cf. tāgā
def: s.m. Thread
s.m.(?), A combination of three short syllables.
s.m. = dhaggaṛ, q.v.
s.f. An adulteress ( = dhagaṛ-bāz).
s.f. An adulteress ( = dhagaṛ-bāz).
v.n. To burn brightly; to glow; to shine, glitter, flash.
s.m. = ḍaggā, q.v.
s.m. Paramour, lover, gallant, leman; — master, superior, lord
subs: (or baḵẖshī-) kā dhaggaṛ, lit. 'The Qāẓī's (or, the pay-master's, or collector's) master,' I care not a fig for the Qāzī's (or, the collector's) master (much less for the Qāzī himself); a fig for parson or squire; I am independent of judge (or paymaster).

def: v.n. To roll, to wallow.
s.m. Paramour, lover, gallant, leman; — master, superior, lord
subs: (or baḵẖshī-) kā dhaggaṛ, lit. 'The Qāẓī's (or, the pay-master's, or collector's) master,' I care not a fig for the Qāzī's (or, the collector's) master (much less for the Qāzī himself); a fig for parson or squire; I am independent of judge (or paymaster).

def: s.f. Sound, echo, noise, report, ring; cry, groan, yell
subs: To cry, groan, to raise an outcry, to yell.

def: s.m. Steep slope, steep bank (of a river, &c.)
v.t. To throw down, &c. ( = ḍhānā, q.v.); to cause (one) to fall, to bring down, to humble.
s.m. = ḍhaiyā or ḍahiyā, q.v.
v.t. = ḍhānā, q.v.
v.n. To fall, fall down, &c. ( = ḍhanā, q.v.)
subs: or ḍhahe-paṛnā, or ḍhah-jānā, v.n. (intens.) To fall down, tumble down, fall in, to sag, settle; to be demolished, razed, or destroyed; to stay, remain.

ety: Prk. अड्ढाइआ = अड्ढअइआ = अड्ढअइज्जा = S. अर्द्ध+तृतीया
def: adj. Two and a half ( = aṛhāʼī)
subs: 'To meet with death in two and a half hours,' to die very soon (a phrase used by women).

def: s.f. A wet-nurse, a foster-mother; a nurse; a midwife ( = dāʼī)
subs: v.t. To put out to nurse.

ety: prob. fem. of ḍhayā, perf. part. of ḍhanā
def: s.f. A strong pull, tug, heave, strain; vigorous and determined effort; staying overlong (as of a guest or visitor)
subs: To make a vigorous and determined effort; to throw (oneself) upon, force (oneself) upon, to force an invitation by sticking in a house till dinner is ready.

def: v.n. = ḍhahnā, q.v.
s.m. Steadiness, firmness, constancy; courage; gravity, &c. ( = dhīr, q.v.)
subs: adj. Assuming firmness or composure, endowed with calmness, steady, calm, composed, cool
To be steady, constant, firm, &c. ( = dhīraj karnā, q.v.)
dhairya-mat, dhairya-mant, or dhairya-vān, dhairya-vat, dhairya-vant, adj. Possessed of firmness, or patience, &c., firm, steady, constant; — patient, forbearing, &c. (see dhīr).

def: s.m. The sixth note of the gamut.
s.m. A measure of two and a half sers.

ety: for ḍhayā, perf. part. of ḍhanā
def: part. adj. Fallen, ruined, destroyed, &c.; — s.m. Land near a village, field; — a tribe of Jāṭs (cf. ḍhe)
subs: adj. Ruined, laid waste, destroyed, desolated.

def: s.f. Air, mien, appearance; shape, form, figure, stature, person; style, fashion, dress; posture, position, attitude; — (S. ध्वजः), s.m. Organ of generation, penis
subs: To change the appearance, or shape, or dress; to change the attitude in fencing.

def: s.m. Posture, attitude ( = dhaj).
s.f. Flag, banner, ensign, standard, pennon, a slip of cloth; a pole with a strip of cloth attached (which is erected, in fulfilment of a vow near some place of worship).
adj. (f. -ī), Well-formed, well-shaped, well-made; well-looking, personable; well-dressed, stylish.
s.f. = dhajjī, q.v.
s.f. A slip (of cloth or paper), strip, shred, rag, tatter
subs: v.n. 'To become a shred'; to become very thin, or weak or feeble
dhajjiyāṅ karnā (-kī), To tear to shreds; to punish severely; to tear (one's) character to pieces, to disgrace, to expose to infamy
v.n. To be in tatters; to be reduced to poverty.

def: s.f. Renown, fame; grandeur, glory, pomp; — awe, dread, fear, terror
subs: (-kī), The fame (of a place, &c.) to be established, to be famed, become famous; to be held in awe, be regarded with dread.

def: s.f. A post.
s.m. = ḍhāk, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. दक्षि or धक, rt. दह्
def: s.m. The Butea frondosa (syn. palās. The tree in full bloom presents a striking spectacle, like fire on the horizon; from natural fissures and incisions made in the bark, issues, during the hot season, a red juice, which soon hardens into a ruby-coloured, brittle, astringent gum, similar to kino, and sold as Bengal kino; lac is collected on the branchlets; the leaves are used as plates, &c., and instead of paper, to wrap up parcels, and are given as fodder to buffaloes; the flowers, with alum, are made into the yellow dye used in the Holī festival, and are also used medicinally; the seeds are given as purgative and anthelmintic, mostly in veterinary practice; the wood is used for coating wells, and for the sacrificial fire)
subs: s.m. The person who collects the gum from ḍhāk trees.

def: s.m. A weight.
s.m. A thick wood of ḍhāk trees; the district and city of Dhākā (Anglice, Dacca).
s.m. = H ڐهکلنا ढकलना ḍhakalnā, v.t. (dialec.) = ḍhakel; ḍhakelnā, qq.v.
v.t. = H ڐهکل ढकल ḍhakal, s.m. (dialec.) = ḍhakel; ḍhakelnā, qq.v.

ety: dhakkā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. = dhakkā denā, q.v.s.v. dhakkā.
v.t. = ḍhakwānā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Of mixed origin or breed, mongrel; — a mongrel, cross-breed (syn. dog̠lā).
s.f. corr. of ḍakār, q.v.
s.m. Name of a small tribe of Rājpūts in the district of Agra.
v.n. corr. of ḍakārnā, q.v.
s.m. (f.?), Beating, palpitation, tremor; perturbation, anxiety, apprehension ( = dha-ṛak); — adv. Pit-a-pat

ety: dhakdhak˚, by redupl. fr. dhak, q.v.; cf. dhaṛaknā
def: v.n. To blaze up, to burn brightly; — to shine, glitter, flash, twinkle; — to beat, throb, palpitate, flutter; to tremble, shake (through fear); to be perturbed, be anxious.
s.f. Beating, palpitation; perturbation, &c. ( = dhak-dhak); consideration, reflection
subs: (com. dhugdhugī), s.f. The hollow in the throat below the Adam's apple; an ornament (as a small mirror set in gold or silver, &c.) worn round the neck (usually resting on the depression below the Adam's apple; it is said to take its name from its rising and falling with the action of the heart)

ety: dhakā ( = dhakkā) + Prk. इल्लो = S. इलः
def: s.f. A truck, trolley (syn. ṭhelā).
s.m. = dhakel, q.v.
s.m. = dhakelū, q.v.
v.t. = dhakelnā, q.v.
v.t. T shove, push, jostle ( = dhakk denā)
subs: v.t. To push or shove down, to prec pitate, to push, &c. ( = dhakelnā).

def: adj. & s.m. Shoving, pushing, jostling; — one who shoves, pusher; — a paramour ( = dhaggaṛ).
s.m. = dhakelū, q.v.
adv. Pushing along; — plentifully, abundantly, in heaps.
s.m. = ḍhāk, q.v.
See dhāk, and dhākhā.

ety: S. धाक or धायक+कः, rt. धा
def: s.m. A swing.
s.m. = ḍhākhā, ḍhāk, q.v.
s.m. 1˚ = ḍhāk; 2˚ = ḍhākā, qq.v.

ety: dhakī (fem. of dhakkā)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. = dhakkā denā, q.v.
s.m. Shock, sudden impression of terror (see dhak-dhak)
subs: or ho-jānā, or dhak-se rah-jānā, v.n. To become motionless or paralyzed through fear, to be struck with amazement or consternation, to stand or remain aghast.

def: s.m. A large drum, a double drum.
s.m. Shove, push, knock, jostle, jolt, jog, shock, impact, collision; a blow or stroke of misfortune, or affliction, &c.; loss (in trade), damage
subs: s.m., dhakā-pelī, s.f., dhakkā-dhakkī, s.f., or dhakkam-dhakkā, s.m., Shoving and pushing, jostling
To push and shove, to jostle
(-ko), To shove, push, jolt, jostle, knock against, to thrust or push away; to inflict a blow or shock on, to bring misfortune on
v.n. To receive a shock or blow, to be pushed, shoved, or knocked, &c., to suffer collision
To be pushed or jostled about, to suffer indignities, to be roughly handled; to suffer misfortune
(-ko), To get a knock, or shock, or jolt, &c.; to suffer loss; to meet with misfortune
To push through life somehow, to live with difficulty.

def: s.m. See s.v. dhakkā.
s.m. Covering; cover, lid, pot-lid ( = ḍhapnā).
s.m. Covering; cover, lid, pot-lid ( = ḍhapnā).
v.t. To cover; to conceal, &c. ( = ḍhāṅknā); — to coincide with, fit exactly on or over; — v.n. To be covered; to be concealed
s.f. A small cover, cover, lid, pot-lid.
s.m. Stuffing, padding, superfluity, anything unnecessary; a thing intended to beguile, an idle tale, fiction, fable, delusion, deception.
v.t. To eat or drink immoderately or greedily, to devour, to stuff or cram down, to guzzle

ety: doub. caus. of ḍhāṅknā, or ḍhaknā
def: v.t. To cause to be covered, to have or get covered, &c.

s.m. Cast, mould, form, fashion; manner, mode, way, style; custom, habit, bent; natural condition, state; — inclination, slope, declivity: a gradient; — postpn. After the custom or manner, &c. (of, -ke or -kī?)
subs: v.n. To move or walk slowly.

def: s.f. Shield and sword
s.m. A stream, current; — a kind of cess (about one ānā in the rupee) levied on villages (cf. dhār-bāćh).
s.m. (dialec.) = ḍālā, ḍāl, q.v.
s.f. The act of casting, or moulding; — the cost of casting, &c.

ety: ढाल 'shield'+ait = āʼit = Prk. आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन ( = āpi caus. augment+an, term. of the pres. part.)
def: s.m. 'Shield-bearer,' a military retainer armed with sword and buckler; a targetier; a constable, a village watchman.
s.m. = ḍhaliyā, q.v.

ety: ḍhalait, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ and इआ = S. इकः (इन्+कः), and इका
def: adj. Of or belonging to a ḍhalait; — s.f. The office or duty of a ḍhalait.
s.f. Inclining, tilting; rolling, rocking; spilling (cf. ćhalak).

v.n. To run down, to flow; to be poured out, be spilt, be shed; to fall or hang down loosely, be flaccid or loose (cf. laṭaknā); to decline, sink, fade ( = ḍhalnā); to rock; to roll, slide ( = ḍhulaknā)

ety: ḍhalā (fr. ḍhalnā)+ān = Prk. अअणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Slope, declivity ( = ḍhāl).
v.t. 1˚ To cause to run or flow, to pour out, to cast (metal), to mould; — 2˚ = ḍhalwānā, q.v.

r: ḍhalāʼū, ḍhulāʼū, ḍhalā, nā, ḍhul ā, āʼū
def: adj. Slant, sloping, inclined; — ḍhalāʼū, s.m. Slope, inclination, declivity ( = ḍhalāʼo).
v.n. To gush out, to rush out with a noise, to gurgle.
adj. & s.m. Bearing or armed with a shield; — one armed with a shield, shield-bearer.
s.f. (dialec.) = ḍālī, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhaliyā or ḍhalaiyā, q.v.
s.m. One who pours out; one who casts, or moulds, caster, founder.
s.m. Causing to run over, spilling; — an affection of the eye causing it to run; a blear-eye; — adj. Blear-eyed.
v.t. To cause to run over or down, to cause to flow; to spill; to pour out; to tilt, overturn; to cause to roll, to roll ( = ḍhulkānā)
s.m. A lump of clay, a clod.

ety: by redupl. of rt. ḍhal of ḍhalnā
def: adj. Shaking, tottering, staggering, unsteady, wavering, vacillating (cf. ḍagmag).
v.n. To move from side to side, to sway, totter, stagger, reel, rock; to be unsteady, to waver, vacillate (cf. ḍagmagānā).

v.n. To be poured out or down; to be cast (as metal), be moulded; to run, run down, flow, drop or fall (from, -se); to spill; to roll; to incline ( = ḍhulnā); to decline, sink, fade (as day, or life, &c.)
subs: v.n. (intens.), To run down, flow down, drop or fall down, &c.

def: v.t. To pour out, shed, spill, tilt; to throw, cast; to cast (metal in fusion), to mould, fashion, form, shape, coin (money), frame or compose (a sentence, &c.); to aim or direct (speech, at), to speak (at, -par); (fig.) to do mischief (to), to injure, mar, spoil.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Rolling; falling; moving, slipping, shifting, &c.
subs: lit. 'Shifting and moving shadow'; the changeable state of worldly concerns, the vicissitudes of life or fortune; (fig.) a person of capricious or unsteady temper.

def: adj. & s.m. Sloping, slant ( = ḍhālwāṅ, ḍhalwāṅ); — casting, moulding; — doing mischief, spoiling; — caster, moulder, fashioner, &c.; spoiler; destroyer; — slope, descent (of a hill, &c.)
adj. 1˚ = next, q.v.; — 2˚ Cast (as metal, i.q. ḍhalā).

ety: ḍhālū+S. कः; — or ḍhāl(nā)+Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य + कं
def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Sloping, slanting, declivous ( = ḍhalwāṅ); — cast, moulded ( = ḍhālā).

ety: ḍhal(nā) or ḍhul(nā)+Prk. अअं (with w inserted) = S. अ+कं
def: adj. (f. -wīṅ), Inclined, slant, sloping, declivous
v.t. To cause to be poured out, to have or get poured out; — to cause to be cast or moulded, to have or get cast, &c.
s.f. Sound, loud noise or report (as of a drum, &c.); thud
subs: s.f. Noise, tumult, bustle, riot, row
s.m.(f.?), Sound of walking over a hollow place, or of stamping, or of continued beating or thumping, or drumming, &c.; — adv. With a loud noise, with a bang, thump-thump, drum-drum, &c.
s.f. Sound of a drum, &c.; — 2˚ = dham-ā-dham
adv. With a loud noise or report, with a thud, noisily; heavily; suddenly, all at once.

def: adj. & s.m. The double of any number or quantity together with its half (e.g. 57½ as 2½ times 23), 2½ times any number; — the multiplication table of 2½ times.
s.m. A basket (made of cane).
s.m. Dwelling-place, house, abode, home, residence, place; paradise, heaven; splendour, &c. (see dhāman); name of a class of superhuman beings
subs: vulg. dhāmad, adj. & s.m. Heaven-giving; — a person by whom, or the means by which, paradise is obtained
dhāma-vant, dhām-vān, adj. Splendid, luminous; eminent, exalted, illustrious, heroic.

def: adj. & s.m. The double of any number or quantity together with its half (e.g. 57½ as 2½ times 23), 2½ times any number; — the multiplication table of 2½ times.
s.f. = H دهمادهم धमधम dhamā-dham, s.m. See s.v. dham.
s.m. = H دهماچوکڙي धमाचौकड़ीdhāmā-ćaukṛī, s.f. See s.v. dham.
s.m. (f. -ikā), A blacksmith.

s.f. Noise of footsteps overhead, or such as arises from the fall of a body, sound of thumping, drumming, &c.; — shooting pain, beating, throbbing, throb, pulsation; glow, blast, hot blast; lustre, radiance, glitter; — threatening, threat; awe; — fall, or unevenness (in ground, such as would produce a shock; e.g., in kahār's idiom, dhamak hai).
s.m. A loud report (as of a falling wall, or a gun, &c.), crack, crash, bang; — a hard blow or thump; — a kind of cannon (carried on an elephant); a firelock.

ety: dhamak˚ = Prk. धमक्क(इ), or धमक्के(इ), fr. S. धम+कृ
def: v.n. To shoot with pain, to throb; to beat, palpitate, thump; to glow; to flash, glimmer; — to make a rush; to make a hasty inroad or incursion; to run (against); cf. dhar-dhamaknā (in this last sense dhamaknā would seem to point to S. dhāv as its source)
subs: v.n. To throb, &c.; — to resound, ring again.

def: s.m. A qalandar or calendar who leaps into, or runs through, fire.
s.m. Dwelling, house, &c. ( = dhām, q.v.); — a troop, host; — state, dignity, majesty, glory; light, splendour, radiance, brilliancy.

s.m. A large species of snake (which is said to suck cows, and to be harmless); — a kind of wood; a kind of bamboo, Grewia tiliœfolia (of which bows are made); — dhāman, A grass of good quality (found in the Bhaṭṭī territory).
s.f. A small tube through which to blow a fire; any tubular vessel of the body, as an artery, a vein, or a nerve.
s.f. Any tubular vessel of the body; a vein.
s.m. = dhamāl, q.v.
adj. See s.v. dhām.
s.f. Stamping overhead; drumming noise or sound; thumping, banging.
v.n. To produce a noise by running over a hollow place, to make a noise by stamping, &c.; to stamp repeatedly; to drum; to thump.
adj. Fat, corpulent ( = dhumpāl).
s.f. dim. of dhāmā, q.v.; — also = dhāmin, q.v.
s.f. A blacksmith's wife.
(pl. of dhāmī?), prop. n. Name of a sect, followers of Prānāth, a Hindū reformer who flourished in the seventeenth century in Bunḍelkhanḍ.
s.m. Sound produced by the fall of a heavy body; a shock; a thump, blow, buffet; — hot blast; closeness and heat (as after rain); feverishness; — threatening, chiding, threat, menace.
s.f. Threatening, &c. ( = dhamkī, q.v.).
v.t. To chide, scold, threaten, menace, snub, cow, daunt; to repress by threats, or reproof, &c.
s.f. Threatening, threat, menace, reprimand, snubbing
subs: (-kī), To be frightened or coerced by the threats (of another).

def: adj. & s.m. Glowing; bright, glittering; — a bright colour.
v.n. To roll, totter, stagger, reel, &c. ( = ḍhalmalānā, and ḍagarnā).

s.m. Jumping into, or running through fire (a practice of faqīrs or qalandars; cf. dam-madār); skipping and capering (of qalandars, &c.); — a musical measure; a piece composed in that measure; a kind of song (sung during the Holī festival); wild and tumultuous merriment, noise, tumult, uproar; — adj. (dialec.), Bulky, big, fat, corpulent ( = dhumpāl, q.v.).
s.m. A pavior's monkey, a beetle, heavy rammer or beater (with a vertical handle)
subs: v.t. To ram or beat down (a terrace, or floor, or new-laid turf).

ety: dham˚ = Prk. धम(इ) = S. धम(ति), rt. ध्मा
def: v.n. To be grieved; to be amazed or astounded.
s.m. A kind of tambourine.
s.m. corr. of ḍhap, q.v.
s.m. A thump, blow, buffet.
s.m. The rice plant; rice in the husk; a sort of fire-work (rice husks filled with powder and attached to a small stick)
subs: s.m. lit. 'Rice-leaf'; (fig.), a slender or delicate object or creature; — adj. Slender, slim, thin, delicate
s.f. Rice-cutting, reaping the rice crop
s.m. A pounder or thresher of rice
s.f. Pounding rice to separate it from the husk, threshing rice

def: s.m. Property (of any description), chattels, substance, wealth, treasure, riches, money, gold, wealth in cattle or herds, cattle; fortune, good fortune, prosperity (in this sense prob. fr. S. dhanya)
subs: (˚na+adh˚), s.m. Title or right to property
(˚na+adh˚), s.m. (f. -inī), One who has a right or title to property, an heir
(˚na+adh˚), s.m. An overseer of treasure, a treasurer; an epithet of Kuvera
(˚na+āḍh˚), adj. Abounding in wealth, very rich or wealthy, opulent
s.f., or dhanāḍhyatva, s.m. Wealthiness, opulence
(˚na+ar˚), adj. & s.m. Wealth-seeking, desiring wealth, covetous, greedy, miserly; — an avaricious person; a beggar
(˚na+ar˚), s.m. Acquisition of property or wealth
(na+āśā), s.f. Longing after wealth, desire of riches
(˚na+āg˚), s.m. lit. 'Dwelling or treasury of wealth'; a very rich man
s.f. The science of political economy
s.m. 'Lord of riches or treasure'; an epithet of Kuvera; — a very wealthy person
s.f. An avaricious woman; — the plant or vegetable Ardisia solanacea
s.f. Thirst for riches, covetousness, greed
(S. dhana+trayodaśī), s.f. The thirteenth day of the waning moon of the month Áśvin or Kārtik, and the festival held on that day (on which the Shroffs worship money)
s.m. A herd of horned cattle
dhan-dāyī, dhana-da, vulg. dhanad (f. -dā), adj. Granting wealth, giving money or property, munificent, liberal, beneficent; — s.m. An epithet of Kuvera
adj. Proud of (one's) riches or wealth, purse-proud
s.m. Desire of wealth, avarice, greed, covetousness
adj. Intoxicated with wealth, inflated with the pride of wealth, purse-proud; — s.m. Pride or intoxication from wealth
adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Wealthy, rich, opulent, affluent; — a wealthy man (syn. daulat-mand)
s.m. 'The spending of money,' expenditure, extravagance
s.m. lit. 'Stealing property'; a thief, pilferer
s.f. A kind of perfume (commonly called ćor)
adj. Deprived of wealth, reduced to poverty, poor
(˚na+īś), s.m. 1˚ = dhaneśvar; — 2˚, the horn-bill, Buceros ( = dhan-ćiṛī)
(˚na+īś˚), s.m. 'Lord of treasure,' Kuvera, the god of wealth; — a very rich man.

def: adj. Fortunate, &c. ( = dhanya, q.v.); — intj. Well done! bravo! what happiness! how fortunate! blessings (on you)! — s.m. Thanks
subs: (-kā), To express devout thanks, to be grateful (for)
adj. Thankful, grateful; — intj. Well done! bravo! — dhan-naṣīb = dhan-bhāg, q.v.

def: s.m. = دهنو dhanu, q.v.
s.m. Hedge, fence, enclosure.

ety: dhā˚ = Prk. धा(इ) = S. धाव(ति), rt. धाव्
def: v.n. To run, go quickly, make haste, to rush; to bustle about, to toil, labour, drudge.
v.t. To worship, adore; to invoke.
s.f. = dhān
s.m. The portion of a village inhabited by Gonds (such portion being separated from the rest).
s.m. = دهانا dhānā, q.v.
s.f. A wife, mistress, lady, ( = dhannī, q.v.).

ety: ḍhā˚, prob. fr. S. धस्; or fr. द्राह्; cf. ḍhāhnā, and ḍhālnā
def: v.t. To break, batter, shatter, throw down, pull down, knock down, raze (a building, &c.), destroy, demolish; to cause to befall, to bring (trouble, &c. upon)
subs: (-kī), 'To throw or pull down the ready-made structure (of),' to overthrow the project (of), spoil or mar (one's) plans.

ety: fr. the trans. ḍhāhnā; cf. ḍhahnā
def: v.n. To fall, fall down, fall in, tumble to pieces; to become dilapidated; to be demolished, razed, or destroyed
subs: or ḍah-jānā, v.n. intens. of and = ḍhanā.

def: adj. See s.v. dhan.
adj. = S دهنارجن धनार्जन dhanārjan, s.m. See s.v. dhan.
s.m. = dhān-waiyā, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. धूनि (rt. धू)+कृ; cf. dhunaknā
def: v.n. To be set on fire, be kindled; to blaze.

r: dhanan-jay, lit, Terminalia, Pentaptera, Arjuna, Plumbago zeylanica
def: s.m. lit. 'Conquering booty,' 'wealth-acquiring'; — fire, and its deity Agni; an epithet of Arjun; — the plants Terminalia (or Pentaptera) Arjuna, and Plumbago zeylanica.
adj. = dhanattar, q.v.
s.m. = dhanattar and dhanvantari, qq.v.
s.m. = dhanattar and dhanvantari, qq.v.
s.m. = S دهناڐهيه धनाढ्य dhanāḍhya, adj. See s.v. dhan.
s.f. Name of a Rāginī or musical mode; (fig.) a stinging or felling blow; — a trick in wrestling.
s.f. = dhanyatā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Very rich or wealthy, opulent, powerful; — a wealthy person, a great or influential man.
s.m. Name of a physician in the court of Indra; — a very skilful physician.
s.m. Frame, frame-work; skeleton; mould (syn. sāṅćā); plan, form (syn. naqsha).
s.m. Frame, frame-work; skeleton; mould (syn. sāṅćā); plan, form (syn. naqsha).
s.m. = dhandhā, q.v.
s.f. = dhāṅdhal. q.v.

ety: prob. S. ध्वस्त + तेल; cf. ḍhahnā
def: s.m. Scum of oil or ghī; oil in which anything has been fried.
s.m. Craft, calling, trade, occupation, business, avocation, employment, work, way of life.
s.m. = dhandhā, q.v.

ety: S. द्वन्द्व+al = Prk. अडी = S. ली
def: s.f. Wrangling, altercation, quarrel, row, disturbance; subterfuge, trick, cheating, juggle, chicanery ( = dhaṅdhlā)
subs: or dhāṅdhal lānā, or dhāṅdhal maćānā, v.n. To practise trickery, to play false, to cheat, &c.

ety: prob. dhandhā+Prk. आला = S. आला; — but cf. dhandlā
def: s.f. A procuress, a bawd (syn. dallāla; kuṭnī).
s.m. Chicanery, trickery, juggle, cheating, wily practices.
adj. & s.m. Retired, lonely, solitary (syn. udās; ekāntī); — a recluse, a devotee.
s.f. Retirement, loneliness, solitude.
